questions.question: What keyboard shortcut converts a selected range into an Excel Table?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + T opens the Create Table dialog — giving you auto-expanding ranges, structured references, filter buttons and banded rows.

questions.question: Which function removes leading and trailing spaces (and collapses internal double spaces)?
questions.explanation: TRIM handles spaces. CLEAN removes non-printing characters like line breaks and tabs.

questions.question: An export has invisible line breaks and tab characters inside cells. Which function strips those out?
questions.explanation: CLEAN removes non-printable characters. A common combo is =TRIM(CLEAN(A2)).

questions.question: A column of numbers won't SUM and the values are left-aligned with a green triangle. What fixes it?
options: They're stored as text — use VALUE() or Convert to Number
The column is too narrow — widen it
questions.explanation: Left-aligned numbers with a warning triangle are text. VALUE() converts them back (or use the ⚠️ menu → Convert to Number).

questions.question: A date like "2024-03-15" is left-aligned and can't be grouped by month. Which function converts it to a real date?
questions.explanation: DATEVALUE turns a text date into a real Excel date. You'll need real dates to group by month/quarter in Stage 4.

questions.question: An order ID is "ORD-2024-0042". Which formula pulls out just the year, "2024"?
questions.explanation: MID(text, start, length) extracts from the middle — here 4 characters starting at position 5. LEFT would give "ORD-"; RIGHT would give "0042".
